page: Amazon Web Services r5ad.4xlarge is a memory-optimized instance powered by an AMD EPYC 7571 processor. Built on the x86_64 architecture, this instance features 16 vCPUs mapped to 8 physical cores with multithreading enabled. It is equipped with 128.0 GB of DDR4 memory operating at 3 GHz, providing an 8:1 memory-to-vCPU ratio. The instance is managed by the Nitro hypervisor and includes 600 GB of local NVMe SSD instance storage, alongside a baseline network bandwidth of 5 Gbps.

Benchmark data reveals a contrast between compute and memory performance. Single-core CPU performance is relatively low, with a stress-ng single-core score of 590.9 and a Geekbench single-core score of 726.0, both falling into the bottom 10%. Multi-core performance is stronger, achieving a Geekbench multi-core score of 4582.0 and a Passmark CPU score of 12505.34, which are in the average range. Memory performance is mixed; while cached small-block read bandwidth is average at 620.46 GB/sec, uncached large-block write bandwidth is poor at 13.15 GB/sec.

This hardware profile is optimized for workloads requiring high memory capacity and fast local scratch space rather than raw single-threaded compute power. The inclusion of 600 GB of local NVMe SSD storage makes it suitable for database operations, caching layers, and memory-intensive data processing. Passmark database operations and Redis SET benchmarks both perform in the average tier, with Redis achieving 9.89 million operations per second. The use of AMD EPYC processors offers a cost-efficient alternative for memory-heavy applications that do not require the highest single-core clock speeds.
description: Amazon Web Services r5ad.4xlarge is a memory-optimized instance utilizing the Nitro hypervisor and an AMD EPYC 7571 processor. It features 16 vCPUs, 8 physical cores, and 128.0 GB of DDR4 memory, establishing an 8:1 memory-to-vCPU ratio. The instance includes 600 GB of local NVMe SSD storage and a baseline network bandwidth of 5 Gbps. Performance benchmarks indicate average multi-core capabilities, with a Passmark CPU score of 12505.34 and a Geekbench multi-core score of 4582.0, though single-core performance is relatively weak. Memory bandwidth is average for cached small blocks but drops for uncached large blocks. This configuration is qualitatively cost-efficient for memory-intensive workloads that benefit from local NVMe storage, such as database operations, caching layers, and medium-scale data processing, rather than compute-heavy or single-threaded applications.
og_description: Amazon Web Services r5ad.4xlarge is a memory-optimized instance featuring 16 vCPUs, 128 GB DDR4 RAM, and 600 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, designed for database operations and memory-intensive workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services r5ad.4xlarge is a memory-optimized instance with 16 vCPUs, 128 GB RAM, and 600 GB local NVMe SSD storage.
tagline: A memory-optimized instance featuring dedicated AMD EPYC processors, high-capacity DDR4 memory, and fast local NVMe SSD storage for database workloads.
bullet_points: 16 vCPUs and 128.0 GB of DDR4 memory providing an 8:1 memory-to-vCPU ratio
600 GB of local NVMe SSD instance storage for high-speed scratch space
Powered by an AMD EPYC 7571 processor under the Nitro hypervisor
Average multi-core performance suitable for database and caching operations
